instructions.instruction: Run bash scripts/setup_tools.sh for diagnostics on first-time validation
instructions.original_snippets:
0. Tool Prerequisites Check (RECOMMENDED for first-time validation)
   └─> Run bash scripts/setup_tools.sh for diagnostics
instructions.relevant_when: First-time validation or when tool availability is uncertain

instructions.instruction: Run yamllint before ansible-playbook --syntax-check; fix YAML errors first
instructions.original_snippets: Run `yamllint` then `ansible-playbook --syntax-check`. Fix YAML errors first, then Ansible-specific issues.
instructions.relevant_when: Validating any Ansible playbook or role

instructions.instruction: Run ansible-lint to enforce best practices; use --fix for auto-fixable issues and review before applying
instructions.original_snippets: Run `ansible-lint` to enforce best practices. Use `--fix` for auto-fixable issues; review before applying.
instructions.relevant_when: Validating Ansible playbooks or roles

instructions.instruction: Check for deprecated modules using references/module_alternatives.md
instructions.original_snippets: ├─> Check for deprecated modules (references/module_alternatives.md)
instructions.relevant_when: When ansible-lint or manual review finds deprecated module usage

instructions.instruction: Detect non-FQCN module usage by running bash scripts/check_fqcn.sh
instructions.original_snippets: ├─> Detect non-FQCN usage: bash scripts/check_fqcn.sh
instructions.relevant_when: Validating any Ansible playbook or role

instructions.instruction: Run ansible-playbook --check --diff (not just --check); always use both flags together for dry-run testing
instructions.original_snippets:
NEVER run `--check` mode without `--diff`
...GOOD: `ansible-playbook --check --diff site.yml`
instructions.relevant_when: Performing dry-run testing of Ansible playbooks

instructions.instruction: When molecule/ directory is detected in a role, run bash scripts/test_role.sh <role-path> automatically
instructions.original_snippets: ├─> If present, run bash scripts/test_role.sh <role-path> automatically
instructions.relevant_when: Validating Ansible roles that have a molecule/ directory

instructions.instruction: If molecule fails due to environment (Docker, versions), document the blocker but do not fail overall validation; if it fails due to role code, provide debugging steps
instructions.original_snippets: If molecule fails due to environment (Docker, versions), document the blocker but don't fail overall validation. If molecule fails due to role code, provide detailed debugging steps.
instructions.relevant_when: Running molecule tests in environments that may not have Docker

instructions.instruction: Run bash scripts/extract_ansible_info_wrapper.sh <path> to extract module/collection information
instructions.original_snippets: ├─> Run bash scripts/extract_ansible_info_wrapper.sh <path> to extract info
instructions.relevant_when: When playbooks use custom modules or collections

instructions.instruction: For public collections, use Context7 MCP (mcp__context7__resolve-library-id then get-library-docs) for documentation lookup
instructions.original_snippets:
**For public collections** (community.general, ansible.posix, cisco.ios, etc.):
1. `mcp__context7__resolve-library-id` with collection name
2. `mcp__context7__get-library-docs` for specific module
instructions.relevant_when: When playbooks use public Ansible Galaxy collections

instructions.instruction: For custom/private collections, use WebSearch with format '[collection-namespace].[module-name] ansible version [version] documentation'
instructions.original_snippets:
**For custom/private collections** — use WebSearch:
"[collection-namespace].[module-name] ansible version [version] documentation"
instructions.relevant_when: When playbooks use private or custom Ansible collections

instructions.instruction: Run bash scripts/validate_playbook_security.sh or validate_role_security.sh for Checkov security scanning
instructions.original_snippets: ├─> Run bash scripts/validate_playbook_security.sh or validate_role_security.sh (Checkov)
instructions.relevant_when: Any Ansible validation task

instructions.instruction: Run bash scripts/scan_secrets.sh to detect hardcoded credentials
instructions.original_snippets: └─> Run bash scripts/scan_secrets.sh (detects hardcoded credentials)
instructions.relevant_when: Any Ansible validation task

instructions.instruction: Read references/common_errors.md when errors are detected
instructions.original_snippets: ├─> Read references/common_errors.md when errors detected
instructions.relevant_when: When validation tools produce errors

instructions.instruction: Read references/best_practices.md when warnings are detected
instructions.original_snippets: ├─> Read references/best_practices.md when warnings detected
instructions.relevant_when: When ansible-lint or other tools produce warnings

instructions.instruction: Read references/module_alternatives.md for deprecated or non-FQCN modules
instructions.original_snippets: ├─> Read references/module_alternatives.md for deprecated/non-FQCN modules
instructions.relevant_when: When deprecated modules or non-FQCN usage is detected

instructions.instruction: Read references/security_checklist.md when security issues are found
instructions.original_snippets: └─> Read references/security_checklist.md when security issues found
instructions.relevant_when: When security scanning finds vulnerabilities

instructions.instruction: Never skip ansible-lint because the playbook 'works'; run in CI before every merge and treat violations as build failures
instructions.original_snippets:
NEVER skip `ansible-lint` because the playbook "works"
...GOOD: Run `ansible-lint` in CI before every merge and treat any rule violation as a build failure.
instructions.relevant_when: Any Ansible validation task

instructions.instruction: Never use molecule test as a substitute for ansible-lint; run ansible-lint first (fast), then molecule (slower); both gates required
instructions.original_snippets:
NEVER use `molecule test` as a substitute for `ansible-lint`
...GOOD: Run `ansible-lint` first (fast, catches obvious errors), then Molecule (slower, catches runtime and idempotency errors) — both gates are required.
instructions.relevant_when: Validating Ansible roles with molecule tests

instructions.instruction: Never ignore no-changed-when warnings; add changed_when: false for read-only commands or explicit changed_when condition for state-changing ones
instructions.original_snippets:
NEVER ignore `no-changed-when` lint warnings
...GOOD: Add `changed_when: false` for genuinely read-only commands, or add an explicit `changed_when` condition
instructions.relevant_when: Playbooks with command or shell tasks

instructions.instruction: Never disable validate_certs in get_url or other network tasks
instructions.original_snippets:
# BAD
- get_url:
    url: https://example.com/file.tar.gz
    dest: /tmp/file.tar.gz
    validate_certs: false
instructions.relevant_when: Playbooks using get_url, uri, or other network modules

instructions.instruction: Use Ansible Vault or environment variables for secrets; never hardcode credentials
instructions.original_snippets: **Secrets:** Use Ansible Vault / environment variables; never hardcode credentials
instructions.relevant_when: Playbooks handling passwords, API keys, or sensitive data

instructions.instruction: Always set mode: on file/template tasks
instructions.original_snippets: **File permissions:** Always set `mode:` on file/template tasks; encrypt sensitive files
instructions.relevant_when: Playbooks with file, template, or copy tasks

instructions.instruction: Validation order: YAML syntax → Ansible syntax → Lint → Security scan → Secrets scan → Check mode → Molecule tests
instructions.original_snippets: Validation order: YAML syntax → Ansible syntax → Lint → Security scan → Secrets scan → Check mode → Molecule tests
instructions.relevant_when: Full validation pass on any Ansible playbook or role

instructions.instruction: Pin collection versions in requirements.yml; test before upgrading
instructions.original_snippets: Pin collection versions in `requirements.yml`; test before upgrading
instructions.relevant_when: Playbooks or roles using Galaxy collections
